items.title: Quicksand causes terrifying ‘Indiana Jones moment’ for second Adelaide beachgoer

children.content: Days after a young Adelaide beachgoer had to be rescued from quicksand, a second person has spoken out about a similar experience along the same stretch of coast, saying she was momentarily overcome by "complete fear".

articles.title: 'Panic started to set in': Woman rescued from quicksand on Adelaide beach

children.content: A relaxing sunset walk at the beach with a friend turned to panic for a young woman after her leg got stuck in quicksand, prompting a rescue from police and fire crews.

image.altText: A person wearing jeans shorts, their calf submerged in wet sand

gallery.altText: A woman in a raincoat cuddling her dog.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a raincoat cuddling her dog.

items.title: Weather warning for abnormally high tides along entire SA coastline

children.content: South Australia's State Emergency Service warns people not to risk their safety as abnormally high tides are set to lash the state's coastline.

image.altText: Flooding over a road with a supermarket

items.title: Farmers flock to buy CCTV cameras to stop fuel theft

children.content: Fuel theft is prompting farmers to install CCTV cameras at unprecedented rates, leading one security company to overhaul its entire business model.

image.altText: A man in hi-vis and a cap stands in front of a diesel tank.

items.title: Ex-soldier, comedian and aspiring Martian is the Aussie face of Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.

gallery.altText: A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.
Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of a cave in Laos

items.title: Farm profits to dive by 70 per cent as drought and costs bite

children.content: The ABARES report for the June quarter predicts national crop production will fall by more than 20 per cent, with profits down 70 per cent for broadacre farms.

image.altText: Two hands pull gently on wheat in a paddock.

items.title: Tough times for hospitality businesses with rising cost of food and power

children.content: The hospitality sector is grappling with "unprecedented" cost increases, according to a national peak body, with many being forced to close their doors.

image.altText: A woman leans an arm on a food counter in a restaurant

items.title: Police scour mine shafts, drain dam in search for missing outback woman

children.content: A renewed search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf will include searching about 1,000 mine shafts and draining Blue Dam.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and tattoos on her arm wearing a black singlet smiles while sitting at a bar

items.title: The impact of Australia only buying second-hand submarines under AUKUS

children.content: Australia will only buy second-hand Virginia nuclear-powered submarines from the US during the AUKUS stopgap phase, with some analysts criticising the move and others believing it is a positive change of plan.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Paddle-steamer first launched 120 years ago returns to working life

children.content: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Canally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.

image.altText: The PS Canally is moored on a river. It is white and grey with colourful flags hung up on it.

items.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over golf course redevelopment

children.content: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Adelaide Hills man pleads guilty to murdering mother

children.content: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

image.altText: A bald man with a beard wearing handcuffs and a plaid shirt

items.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone

children.content: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce, including baked goods and bouquets, and business is booming.

image.altText: A woman with stands next to a wooden stall stocked with loaves of bread. She wears jeans and a tshirt, smiling

items.title: What the polls reveal about One Nation's growing appeal

children.content: The One Nation leader, whose popularity has soared in the past 12 months, would be the first to say she doesn't put too much stock in the numbers.

articles.title: Regional voters consider their options as One Nation popularity surges

children.content: Pauline Hanson's right-wing populist party is leading opinion polls for the first time, and regional Queenslanders say they can see why.

image.altText: Man with blue eyes and dark hair looks off centre

items.title: Veteran broadcaster Alan Jones has one of charges against him dropped

children.content: Prosecutors drop one of the criminal charges against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones less than two months before the start of his trial, reducing the number of complainants in his indecent assault case.

image.altText: An old man in a suit walks with his male lawyer amongst a media scrum.

items.title: James Boag ends 145-year history as brewer ceases Tasmanian production

children.content: The famed northern Tasmanian brewer announces it will cease production in the state by November and repay a government loan it secured to keep its Launceston brewery visitor centre open.

image.altText: James Boags Beers 2024-06-25 10:06:00

items.title: Tax deduction red flags on the ATO's radar

children.content: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is warning Australians to stop claiming private expenses on their tax returns. The agency gives its tips this tax time, including the dos and don'ts of claiming work-related expenses and investment losses.

gallery.altText: A tax return form with red flag pins in it

thumbnail.altText: A tax return form with red flag pins in it

items.title: One Nation's David Farley sworn in as Coalition resolves to vote against tax package

children.content: One Nation's David Farley has been sworn in as the MP for Farrer. Meanwhile, the Coalition has resolved to vote against the government's tax package. Follow live.

image.altText: David Farley signs his oath in the lower house.

items.title: Media mogul Antony Catalano has bail varied to allow travel to Byron Bay

children.content: Former ACM executive Antony Catalano will be allowed to visit his wife and kids after successfully applying to have his bail conditions changed.

image.altText: A smiling businessman

items.title: Potential buyers 'happy to pay $23 million' for Whitsundays island

children.content: A proposed resort and overwater gondola at a Whitsundays island is a step closer to becoming reality, after attracting expressions of interest from several possible buyers.

image.altText: A passenger cabin with glass windows is lifted over an island resort by overhead cables.

items.title: Firefighters say they're left with 'what ifs' after fatal housefire

children.content: The United Firefighters Union says the only fire truck at the nearest station had already been called to a job, leading to a delayed response to a fire that killed a three-year-old boy.

image.altText: A man standing near a fire station.

items.title: Owning a dog was nothing like I'd imagined

children.content: I completely neglected to give much thought to whether my personality would slot into a dog's lifestyle. Spoiler — it did not.

items.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything

children.content: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

children.content: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, let alone know how to spell them.

items.title: Twenty richest Australians hold more wealth than bottom 3 million households

children.content: Australia now has 178 billionaires who collectively hold more than $686 billion in wealth, sparking debate about the advantages and disadvantages of the growing number of ultra-wealthy.

thumbnail.altText: moody image of 50- and 100-dollar notes laid out over bank statements

items.title: How batteries muscled out gas and pushed power prices down

children.content: Fossil fuels, which have powered the global economy for centuries, are under threat. And Australia has become ground zero in this global battle.

image.altText: Solar panels on rooftop

items.title: Canberra's new hospital to be 'state-of-the art' — with extra parking

children.content: The ACT government unveils details of the new North Canberra Hospital as part of its latest budget, with the total cost of the project now expected to be $1.5 billion.

image.altText: Early artist impression in grey scale of the new clinical services building

items.title: Neighbours complain about 200 chopper flights a week into luxury resort

children.content: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.

image.altText: A resort surrounded by cliffs.

items.title: Workmate of missing Jack McLennan says he's 'given police absolutely everything'

children.content: The last known person to see missing Queensland man Jack McLennan alive has broken his public silence amid intense community speculation over his role in Mr McLennan's disappearance.

image.altText: Chris Duke wearing a hi-vis yellow shirt and sunglasses

items.title: French Open boss backs line judges despite controversial missed call

children.content: Despite the other grand slams relying on technology for their line calls, French Open director Amelie Mauresmo is still happy with her human line judges despite a controversial miss costing Casper Ruud.

articles.title: 'Excited to be back': Serena Williams announces return to pro-tennis at 44

children.content: The greatest women's champion of the Open Era is returning to tennis after almost four years away, accepting a wildcard into the doubles at the Queen's Club tournament next week.

image.altText: Serena Williams clenches her fist during a tennis match at Wimbledon.

articles.title: French Open Sabalenka's for the taking as draw opens up for number one

children.content: A series of upsets across Roland Garros leaves world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the box seat to win the claycourt grand slam after beating Naomi Osaka.

image.altText: Aryna Sabalenka blows a kiss to the French Open crowd.

gallery.altText: French Open umpire Louise Azemar Engzell and player Alexander Zverev point at a line on court.

thumbnail.altText: French Open umpire Louise Azemar Engzell and player Alexander Zverev point at a line on court.

items.title: Trump rejects Iranian reports of peace talk suspension

children.content: Iranian state media is reporting that the country's government has halted negotiations with the United States over an end to the war in the Middle East, in protest over Israel's conduct in Lebanon.

image.altText: Smoke rises over a town.

items.title: Race to develop Ebola vaccine as deadly strain of virus spreads

children.content: Nearly 250 people are suspected to have died, and more than 1,100 are infected with the rare strain of Ebola in the DRC and Uganda, though the true spread of the virus is feared to be much wider.

image.altText: Five health care workers wearing PPE, facemasks and eyemasks standing lined up out the front of a home.

items.title: NDAs to be waived for landmark military sexual violence inquiry

children.content: Gag orders imposed on former and current Defence personnel who experienced sexual violence will not be enforced, allowing them to participate in a landmark military sexual violence inquiry.

image.altText: A close-up of an Australian Army patch and an Australian flag patch on an ADF officer's uniform.

items.title: Under-16 e-scooter ban watered down by Qld government

children.content: Under the amended bill, children aged between 12 and 17 will be allowed to ride-mobility devices if they're supervised.

image.altText: A close-up view of e-scooter wheels.
